sections.paragraph:
f. (once masc. signif. 3. Jud. 16:28 [כ׳])-

(1) an anvil (see the root No. 1). Isa 41:7.

(2) the tread of the foot (Tritt), hence a step, a footstep, Psa 17:5, “that my footsteps slide not.” Psa 57:7 119:133 140:5 . Trop. Jud. 5:28 , “the steps of their chariots.” And even a foot with which we tread. Plur. פְּעָמֹות artificial feet, Exo 25:12.-As persons sometimes count by beats of hand or foot, hence-

(3) פַּעַם אַחַת pr. one tread, or stroke is once, Jos 6:3, 11 Jos 6:11, 14 Jos 6:14, and together, Isa 66:8 (comp. the Arabic words دَفْغَةُ, مَرَّةُ, خَطْوَةُ). Dual פַּעֲמַיִם twice, Gen 27:36. Plur. שָׁלשׁ פְּעָמִים thrice, Exo 23:17, etc. כַּמֶּה פְעָמִים how often? 1Ki 22:16. פַּעַם וּשְׁתַּיִם once and again, Neh 13:20. הַפַּעַם (this time) now, Gen 29:35 46:30 Exo 9:27 10:17 . בְּפַעַם … כְּפַעַם now as before (einmal wie das andere) Numb. 24:1 Jud. 16:20 1Sa 20:25. פַּעַם … פַּעַם now-now, Pro 7:12.
